page: Amazon Web Services r8g.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 48 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with one thread per core. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier performance in memory-intensive operations. The instance achieves a Passmark memory score of 3393.03 and a database operations score of 47285.97, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with read-only large block speeds reaching 404.36 GB/sec and write large block speeds at 201.11 GB/sec. Multi-core processing is also strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 23531.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 81875.49. Static web serving throughput is highly efficient at 103.71 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core scoring 1706.6, and text generation for small LLM models is relatively weak at 59.89 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8g.12xlarge leverages the Graviton arm64 architecture to deliver high resource density and memory bandwidth per unit of compute, making it highly efficient for memory-bound workloads compared to traditional x86 alternatives. A key tradeoff is the complete absence of local storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage for persistent data. Additionally, workloads must be compatible with the arm64 instruction set. Given its high memory capacity and top-tier database benchmark results, this instance is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, memory-intensive data processing, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r8g.12xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance powered by a 48-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results show top-tier memory performance, with large-block read bandwidth at 404.36 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 201.11 GB/sec, alongside a strong Geekbench multi-core score of 23531.0. While single-core performance is moderate, its high memory density and exceptional database operations throughput make it highly efficient for memory-bound applications. The r8g.12xlarge is best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and high-throughput web serving workloads.
